content:
Case 20:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 77
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Progressive dyspnea, ascites, and a painful gluteal mass in a patient with CLL
- Vital signs: BP 105/65 mmHg, HR 112/min, RR 22/min, Temp 37.7 °C, SpO₂ 92%
Setting: Acute hematology intake
Capabilities: Rapid bedside assessment with point-of-care imaging and routine laboratory panels is available, but invasive sampling or specialized molecular diagnostics are not yet ready.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
2025-09-23: Presents with progressive dyspnea, abdominal distension, and painful swelling of the left gluteal region with induration over old scars from animal bites. Left leg edema present. Pulmonary exam: diminished breath sounds with basal crackles. Vital signs as listed. Current meds: beclometasone/formoterol inhaler, HCTZ, levothyroxine, vitamins, pantoprazole, amlodipine, aciclovir, allopurinol, bisoprolol, candesartan, atorvastatin, zanubrutinib 160 mg daily.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count with differential"}}

Baseline Labs:
Hb 9.2 g/dL, WBC 41 ×10⁹/L (82% lymphocytes), Plt 78 ×10⁹/L. Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.1, fibrinogen 281 mg/dL, D-dimer 2987 µg/L FEU. Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.4 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, AST 185 U/L, ALT 210 U/L, bilirubin 2.8 mg/dL, LDH 852 U/L, uric acid 7.8 mg/dL, albumin 2.8 g/dL, total protein 5.2 g/dL, NT-proBNP 780 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.02 ng/mL, IgG 359 mg/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "point-of-care ultrasound (chest and abdomen) to assess pleural effusions and ascites"}}

response.request: point-of-care ultrasound (chest and abdomen) to assess pleural effusions and ascites

content:
Initial Imaging:
Chest X-ray: bilateral pleural effusions with basal atelectasis (right > left); Abdominal ultrasound: four-quadrant ascites, multiple enlarged paraaortic and mesenteric nodes up to 6.5 cm, bulky hepatic hilum conglomerate, hepatomegaly with nodular contour, splenomegaly 18.2 × 9.6 cm. Echocardiogram: LVEF 51% with RV dilatation and pulmonary hypertension.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) to evaluate for pulmonary embolism"}}

response.request: CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) to evaluate for pulmonary embolism

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Suspected Richter transformation (aggressive lymphoma arising from CLL) presenting with bulky intraabdominal lymphadenopathy/hepatosplenomegaly, high LDH and cytopenias, with likely malignant pleural effusions/ascites and an extranodal painful left gluteal mass", "differentials": ["Progressive/symptomatic CLL with massive adenopathy and serous effusions", "Venous thromboembolism (DVT/PE) causing dyspnea/pulmonary hypertension and leg edema", "Soft-tissue infection/abscess or necrotizing infection in the left gluteal region (over scarred tissue) with reactive systemic findings", "Decompensated liver disease/portal hypertension (infiltrative vs cirrhosis) causing ascites/effusions"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Stabilize respiratory status: supplemental oxygen, close monitoring; consider therapeutic thoracentesis/paracentesis if worsening hypoxemia/respiratory compromise (with platelet-aware procedural planning)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate urgent diagnostic pathway for Richter transformation when feasible: PET-CT or contrast CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and tissue diagnosis via biopsy of the gluteal mass or an accessible bulky node; send pleural/ascitic fluid for cytology/flow if tapped", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Treat likely gluteal soft-tissue infection while awaiting definitive characterization: start empiric broad-spectrum IV antibiotics and obtain surgical evaluation if concern for deep infection/necrotizing process", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Hold zanubrutinib temporarily and review bleeding/procedural risk given thrombocytopenia and anticipated invasive diagnostics; reassess after diagnostic clarification", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Evaluate/mitigate thromboembolic risk: expedited PE/DVT work-up when available; provide pharmacologic VTE prophylaxis if no contraindication (e.g., platelets >50 \u00d710\u2079/L, no active bleeding) and escalate to therapeutic anticoagulation if VTE is confirmed and platelet count allows", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Suspected Richter transformation (aggressive lymphoma arising from CLL) presenting with bulky intraabdominal lymphadenopathy/hepatosplenomegaly, high LDH and cytopenias, with likely malignant pleural effusions/ascites and an extranodal painful left gluteal mass
differentials: Progressive/symptomatic CLL with massive adenopathy and serous effusions
Venous thromboembolism (DVT/PE) causing dyspnea/pulmonary hypertension and leg edema
Soft-tissue infection/abscess or necrotizing infection in the left gluteal region (over scarred tissue) with reactive systemic findings
Decompensated liver disease/portal hypertension (infiltrative vs cirrhosis) causing ascites/effusions

treatment_plan.action: Stabilize respiratory status: supplemental oxygen, close monitoring; consider therapeutic thoracentesis/paracentesis if worsening hypoxemia/respiratory compromise (with platelet-aware procedural planning)

Initiate urgent diagnostic pathway for Richter transformation when feasible: PET-CT or contrast CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and tissue diagnosis via biopsy of the gluteal mass or an accessible bulky node; send pleural/ascitic fluid for cytology/flow if tapped

Treat likely gluteal soft-tissue infection while awaiting definitive characterization: start empiric broad-spectrum IV antibiotics and obtain surgical evaluation if concern for deep infection/necrotizing process
Hold zanubrutinib temporarily and review bleeding/procedural risk given thrombocytopenia and anticipated invasive diagnostics; reassess after diagnostic clarification

Evaluate/mitigate thromboembolic risk: expedited PE/DVT work-up when available; provide pharmacologic VTE prophylaxis if no contraindication (e.g., platelets >50 ×10⁹/L, no active bleeding) and escalate to therapeutic anticoagulation if VTE is confirmed and platelet count allows

Request additional information or solve.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "FDG PET-CT (or contrast-enhanced CT chest/abdomen/pelvis if PET-CT not available) to characterize disease burden and identify best biopsy target"}}

response.request: FDG PET-CT (or contrast-enhanced CT chest/abdomen/pelvis if PET-CT not available) to characterize disease burden and identify best biopsy target

content:
Extended Evaluations:
CT thorax (2025-09-26): segmental pulmonary embolism right lower lobe with infarction/infarct pneumonia; bilateral pleural effusions. PET-CT (2025-09-30): intensely FDG-avid nodal disease (cervical to pelvic), bulky retroperitoneal/hepatic nodes, avid gluteal mass, uptake in infarct zone. Gluteal biopsy (10-01): diffuse large B-cell lymphoma CD19⁺/CD20⁺/PAX5⁺/CD5⁺/CD23⁻, Ki-67 ~80%. Lumbar puncture: clear CSF, no malignant cells.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Richter transformation: diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (CD19+/CD20+/PAX5+, CD5+, Ki-67 ~80%) with widespread FDG-avid nodal/extranodal disease including painful left gluteal mass, complicated by acute segmental right lower-lobe pulmonary embolism with infarction and bilateral pleural effusions/ascites", "differentials": ["De novo DLBCL arising independently of CLL (vs true Richter transformation)", "Hodgkin-variant Richter transformation", "Progressive CLL with pseudo-transformation (less likely given biopsy-proven DLBCL)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Start therapeutic anticoagulation for acute pulmonary embolism (e.g., LMWH or DOAC if no major drug\u2013drug interactions; platelet-guided dosing/holding if thrombocytopenic; oxygen as needed)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate urgent lymphoma-directed therapy for Richter/DLBCL (chemoimmunotherapy such as R-CHOP or DA-R-EPOCH depending on fitness/comorbidities; consider clinical trial if available)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Tumor lysis prophylaxis and monitoring with chemotherapy (aggressive hydration as tolerated, continue allopurinol and escalate to rasburicase if indicated; frequent electrolytes/uric acid/creatinine)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Complete lymphoma staging and pretreatment evaluation: bone marrow biopsy/flow cytometry, hepatitis B serologies (and start prophylaxis if positive), HIV testing, baseline cardiac assessment review, and document performance status", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Discontinue zanubrutinib when starting intensive lymphoma chemoimmunotherapy (to reduce overlapping toxicity/bleeding/drug-interaction risk) and reassess CLL management after lymphoma response", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Assess need for CNS prophylaxis (CD5+ DLBCL and high disease burden): calculate CNS-IPI and consider intrathecal methotrexate or systemic CNS-directed strategy if high-risk despite negative CSF cytology", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Richter transformation: diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (CD19+/CD20+/PAX5+, CD5+, Ki-67 ~80%) with widespread FDG-avid nodal/extranodal disease including painful left gluteal mass, complicated by acute segmental right lower-lobe pulmonary embolism with infarction and bilateral pleural effusions/ascites
differentials: De novo DLBCL arising independently of CLL (vs true Richter transformation)
Progressive CLL with pseudo-transformation (less likely given biopsy-proven DLBCL)

treatment_plan.action: Start therapeutic anticoagulation for acute pulmonary embolism (e.g., LMWH or DOAC if no major drug–drug interactions; platelet-guided dosing/holding if thrombocytopenic; oxygen as needed)
Initiate urgent lymphoma-directed therapy for Richter/DLBCL (chemoimmunotherapy such as R-CHOP or DA-R-EPOCH depending on fitness/comorbidities; consider clinical trial if available)
Tumor lysis prophylaxis and monitoring with chemotherapy (aggressive hydration as tolerated, continue allopurinol and escalate to rasburicase if indicated; frequent electrolytes/uric acid/creatinine)

Complete lymphoma staging and pretreatment evaluation: bone marrow biopsy/flow cytometry, hepatitis B serologies (and start prophylaxis if positive), HIV testing, baseline cardiac assessment review, and document performance status
Discontinue zanubrutinib when starting intensive lymphoma chemoimmunotherapy (to reduce overlapping toxicity/bleeding/drug-interaction risk) and reassess CLL management after lymphoma response

Assess need for CNS prophylaxis (CD5+ DLBCL and high disease burden): calculate CNS-IPI and consider intrathecal methotrexate or systemic CNS-directed strategy if high-risk despite negative CSF cytology

full dose if platelets \u226550\u00d710^9/L, dose-reduce/hold with transfusion support as needed) and provide oxygen/analgesia as required", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Start first-line Richter/DLBCL systemic therapy urgently: chemoimmunotherapy with anti-CD20 plus anthracycline-based regimen (e.g., R-CHOP for most; escalate to DA-R-EPOCH if high-grade biology such as MYC/BCL2/BCL6 rearrangements or other high-risk features once available); consider clinical trial/venetoclax-containing approach if eligible", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Tumor lysis risk mitigation tailored to volume status: frequent TLS labs and renal monitoring; urate-lowering with rasburicase if high-risk/bulky disease and elevated LDH, otherwise allopurinol; cautious IV fluids with diuretics/close monitoring given effusions/ascites/pulmonary HTN", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Definitive staging/biologic risk work-up to guide regimen and next steps: bone marrow biopsy with flow; FISH/cytogenetics for MYC/BCL2/BCL6, TP53; compare clonality to underlying CLL (IGH/NGS); baseline PET parameters for response assessment", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Medication strategy around BTK inhibitor: stop zanubrutinib during intensive chemoimmunotherapy unless on a protocol specifically combining agents; reassess CLL control after lymphoma treatment response", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Supportive care bundle during treatment: manage symptomatic effusions/ascites with therapeutic drainage as needed; growth factor support with myelosuppressive regimens; transfuse PRN; infection prophylaxis (HBV screening/prophylaxis with anti-CD20, continue HSV prophylaxis, consider PJP prophylaxis if prolonged steroids); consider IVIG replacement given IgG 359 mg/dL and infection risk", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Response-adapted consolidation planning: reassess with interim/end-of-treatment PET; if refractory/early relapse, refer early for cellular therapy eligibility (CAR-T) rather than allogeneic SCT given age/comorbidity profile", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Richter transformation in CLL: biopsy-proven diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (CD19+/CD20+/PAX5+, CD5+, CD23−, Ki-67 ~80%) with widespread FDG-avid nodal and extranodal disease (including left gluteal mass) complicated by acute segmental pulmonary embolism with pulmonary infarction and malignant pleural effusions/ascites
differentials: Clonally unrelated (de novo) DLBCL occurring in a patient with CLL (requires IGH/NGS clonality comparison)
Aggressive CLL with prolymphocytic transformation (less likely given DLBCL histology)
Infectious/inflammatory gluteal mass superimposed on lymphoma (secondary process)

treatment_plan.action: Treat acute PE now: initiate therapeutic anticoagulation with platelet-adjusted strategy (prefer LMWH in malignancy/variable counts; full dose if platelets ≥50×10^9/L, dose-reduce/hold with transfusion support as needed) and provide oxygen/analgesia as required

Start first-line Richter/DLBCL systemic therapy urgently: chemoimmunotherapy with anti-CD20 plus anthracycline-based regimen (e.g., R-CHOP for most; escalate to DA-R-EPOCH if high-grade biology such as MYC/BCL2/BCL6 rearrangements or other high-risk features once available); consider clinical trial/venetoclax-containing approach if eligible

Tumor lysis risk mitigation tailored to volume status: frequent TLS labs and renal monitoring; urate-lowering with rasburicase if high-risk/bulky disease and elevated LDH, otherwise allopurinol; cautious IV fluids with diuretics/close monitoring given effusions/ascites/pulmonary HTN

Definitive staging/biologic risk work-up to guide regimen and next steps: bone marrow biopsy with flow; FISH/cytogenetics for MYC/BCL2/BCL6, TP53; compare clonality to underlying CLL (IGH/NGS); baseline PET parameters for response assessment

Medication strategy around BTK inhibitor: stop zanubrutinib during intensive chemoimmunotherapy unless on a protocol specifically combining agents; reassess CLL control after lymphoma treatment response

Supportive care bundle during treatment: manage symptomatic effusions/ascites with therapeutic drainage as needed; growth factor support with myelosuppressive regimens; transfuse PRN; infection prophylaxis (HBV screening/prophylaxis with anti-CD20, continue HSV prophylaxis, consider PJP prophylaxis if prolonged steroids); consider IVIG replacement given IgG 359 mg/dL and infection risk

Response-adapted consolidation planning: reassess with interim/end-of-treatment PET; if refractory/early relapse, refer early for cellular therapy eligibility (CAR-T) rather than allogeneic SCT given age/comorbidity profile
